title: Building chemical stability with mechanics - Nature Energy
gist: A short research briefing (on work from KIST, Korea) reports that a new high-precision stack-pressure control and dilatometry system identified an optimal mechanical stack-pressure window for lithium-ion battery cells that suppresses key degradation pathways and doubles battery lifetime — reframing cell/pack lifetime as partly a mechanical-engineering, not just chemistry, problem.

themes.theme: Mechanical stack pressure as a lifetime lever

insights.claim: Stack pressure strongly influences degradation pathways in lithium-ion batteries, but its optimal value has remained poorly understood prior to this work.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: Identifies a previously under-optimized engineering variable (mechanical pack/cell pressure) as a lever for battery lifetime, relevant to EV and stationary-storage pack designers, not just cell chemists.
insights.quote: Stack pressure strongly influences degradation pathways in lithium-ion batteries, yet its optimal value remains poorly understood.

insights.claim: Using a high-precision stack-pressure control and dilatometry measurement system, researchers (KIST, Korea Institute of Science and Technology) identified an optimal pressure window that suppresses key degradation pathways and doubles battery lifetime.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
entities: Korea Institute of Science and Technology
insights.so_what: A 2x lifetime improvement from mechanical pressure control alone (without new chemistry) would be a low-cost, retrofit-friendly lever for cell/pack manufacturers to extend warranty life and reduce degradation-driven replacement/warranty costs.
insights.quote: a high-precision stack pressure control and dilatometry system reveals an optimal pressure window that suppresses key degradation pathways and doubles battery lifetime

top_entities: Korea Institute of Science and Technology
